text.title: Seven PMBoK 8 Performance Domains
text.summary: Canonical list of the seven PMBoK 8 domains with artefacts, outcomes, and integrated areas; plus the PMBoK 7 to 8 transition delta.

rule.statement: Always use exactly these seven domain names — never add an 8th, never reintroduce "Integration Management", "Quality", or "Communications" as standalone domains.
rule.rationale: PMBoK 8 explicitly integrates Quality, Communications, and Procurement. LLMs trained on PMBoK 6 data will hallucinate these as standalone domains; the canonical seven must be pinned in system prompts.

list.item: Governance — oversight, decision frameworks, value realisation; replaces Integration Management; includes sustainability as a core metric
Scope — define and control what is included and excluded; enhanced definition and traceability
Schedule — plan and manage timelines, milestones, and dependencies
Finance — budgets, costs, financial performance; replaces Cost Management; pairs with EVM
Stakeholders — identify, analyse, and engage all stakeholders; now includes Communications
Resources — acquire and manage team members and physical resources; enhanced focus on team development
Risk — identify, analyse, and respond to uncertainties including threats and opportunities

section.title: Key Artefacts per Domain
section.p: Minimal artefact set that satisfies each domain. A missing artefact is a risk in the Risk domain.

list.item: Principles: 12 (PMBoK 7) → 6 streamlined (PMBoK 8): Stewardship and Ethics, Collaboration, Value Optimisation, Systems and Complexity, Adaptability, Sustainability

Domains: 8 (PMBoK 7) → 7 (PMBoK 8): Team + Project Work collapsed into Resources + Governance; Planning + Delivery merged into Scope/Schedule/Finance; Measurement absorbed into respective domains; Uncertainty → Risk
Sustainability: mentioned in PMBoK 7, core principle in PMBoK 8 — needs a measurable proxy (e.g. CO2 per release, accessibility pass rate)
AI Coverage: none in PMBoK 7, dedicated appendix in PMBoK 8
